name: ViewSettings
summary: Contains static methods and properties to control view settings.

returns.doc: A new view state with current settings.
A new view state with factory settings.

methods.name: RestoreDefaults
methods.summary: Updates from the default setting state.

methods.name: UpdateFromState
methods.summary: Updates from a particular setting state.

params.name: state
params.doc: The new state that will be set.

properties.name: AlwaysPanParallelViews
properties.summary: Gets or sets the 'always pan parallel views' value. If the view is not looking straight at the construction plane, then sets parallel viewports so they will not rotate.

properties.name: AutoAdjustTargetDepth
properties.summary: Gets or sets the 'Auto adjust camera target after Pan and Zoom' value.

properties.name: DefinedViewSetClippingPlanes
properties.summary: Gets or sets the 'Named views set clipping planes' value.

properties.name: DefinedViewSetCPlane
properties.summary: Gets or sets the 'Named views set CPlane' value. When true, restoring a named view causes the construction plane saved with that view to also restore.

properties.name: DefinedViewSetDisplayMode
properties.summary: Gets or sets the 'Named views set display mode' value.

properties.name: DefinedViewSetProjection
properties.summary: Gets or sets the 'Named views set projection' value. When true, restoring a named view causes the viewport projection saved with the view to also restore.

properties.name: LinkedViewports
properties.summary: Gets or sets the 'linked views' activated setting. true enables real-time view synchronization. When a standard view is manipulated, the camera lens length of all parallel projection viewports are set to match the current viewport.

properties.name: PanPlanParallelViewsWithControlShiftRMB
properties.summary: Gets or sets the 'Pan plan parallel views with Control+Shift+RMB' value.

properties.name: PanReverseKeyboardAction
properties.summary: Gets or sets if panning with the keyboard is reversed. false, then Rhino pans the camera in the direction of the arrow key you press. true, then Rhino pans the scene instead.

properties.name: PanScreenFraction
properties.summary: Gets or sets the faction used as multiplier to pan the screen.

properties.name: RotateCircleIncrement
properties.summary: Gets or sets the rotation increment. When the user rotates a view with the keyboard, Rhino rotates the view in steps. The usual step is 1/60th of a circle, which equals six degrees.

properties.name: RotateReverseKeyboard
properties.summary: Gets or sets the rotation direction. If true, then Rhino rotates the camera around the scene, otherwise, rotates the scene itself.

properties.name: RotateToView
properties.summary: Gets or sets the rotation reference. If true, then the views rotates relative to the view axes; false, than relative to the world x, y, and z axes.

properties.name: RotateViewAroundAutogumball
properties.summary: Gets or sets the 'Rotate view around auto gumball' value.

properties.name: RotateViewAroundObjectAtMouseCursor
properties.summary: Gets or sets the 'Rotate view around object at mouse cursor' value.

properties.name: SingleClickMaximize
properties.summary: Gets or sets the 'single-click maximize' value. When true, maximizing a viewport needs a single click on the viewport title rather than a double-click.

properties.name: ThreePointPerspectiveLensLength
properties.summary: Gets the three point perspective lens length. The default is 50mm.

properties.name: TwoPointPerspectiveLensLength
properties.summary: Gets the two point perspective lens length. The default is 20mm.

properties.name: ViewRotation
properties.summary: Gets or sets the view rotation value.

properties.name: ZoomExtentsParallelViewBorder
properties.summary: Border amount to apply to parallel viewport during zoom extents

properties.name: ZoomExtentsPerspectiveViewBorder
properties.summary: Border amount to apply to perspective viewport during zoom extents

properties.name: ZoomScale
properties.summary: Gets or sets the step size for zooming with a wheeled mouse or the Page Up and Page Down keys.
